blocks.text: DTC P0717: Torque converter Turbine Speed Sensor circuit Short circuit or disconnection

The torque converter turbine speed sensor is mounted on the transmission case. The torque converter turbine speed sensor detects the rotation of the speed-sensing gear on the input shaft and generates a voltage signal with a frequency that varies with the speed. This signal is sent to the PGM-FI ECU and used to measure the torque converter turbine speed. The PGM-FI ECU detects missing torque converter turbine speed sensor pulses, etc. during turbine rotation as a failure. Failures are caused by open or temporary disconnections or short circuits. If there is an abnormality in the detected rpm ratio of the torque converter turbine speed sensor and the driven pulley speed sensor, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores a DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

When the speed detected by the torque converter turbine speed sensor [torque converter turbine speed] is less than the speed detected by the driven pulley speed sensor [driven pulley rpm] multiplied by a factor of 0.1 for more than 15 seconds.

items.text: Ground fault in cord (NT line) between PGM-FI ECU and torque converter turbine speed sensor
Disconnection of cord (NT line) between PGM-FI ECU and torque converter turbine speed sensor (including disconnected or loose couplers)
Disconnection of cord (VCC line) between PGM-FI ECU and torque converter turbine speed sensor (including disconnected or loose couplers)
Disconnection of cord (SG line) between PGM-FI ECU and torque converter turbine speed sensor (including disconnected or loose couplers)
Torque converter Turbine Speed sensor failure
Vehicle speed [vehicle speed] 30 km/h or more for at least 15 seconds.
When conducting test drives, the driver must comply with all laws and regulations, observe good manners, and drive in accordance with the surrounding conditions.

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the D indicator light flashes without the PGM-FI warning light illuminating, and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.
Use of the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or removal of the 12V battery terminal will clear the D indicator light, temporary DTC, DTC, and freeze data.
